stem: The student wants to explain why the Lumière brothers' achievement was particularly significant. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The Lumière brothers held the first public film screening in Paris in 1895, showing everyday scenes like workers leaving a factory.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Although Thomas Edison had developed the Kinetoscope in 1891, it allowed only one viewer at a time, whereas the Lumière brothers' 1895 screening made film a shared public experience.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Thomas Edison developed a film viewing device called the Kinetoscope in 1891, four years before the Lumière brothers held their screening in Paris.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The Lumière brothers showed films of everyday scenes in 1895, while Edison's earlier Kinetoscope required viewers to look through a peephole.

explanation: Choice B most effectively accomplishes the goal of explaining why the Lumière brothers' achievement was particularly significant. It establishes the crucial contrast: while Edison's technology came first chronologically, it was limited to individual viewing, whereas the Lumière brothers enabled film to become a collective, public experience. The phrase 'shared public experience' synthesizes the significance—this wasn't just another film device, but a fundamentally different way of experiencing film that would define cinema as we know it. The subordinating conjunction 'Although' appropriately positions Edison's earlier work as context while maintaining focus on what made the Lumière brothers' contribution distinctive and important.

distractorRationale.A: This choice simply reports facts from the notes without establishing any significance. It mentions the screening and the content of the films but provides no context or comparison that would explain why this achievement mattered. The student's goal requires explaining significance, not just describing what happened.
distractorRationale.C: This choice focuses on the wrong element—the chronological relationship between the two developments. While it accurately notes that Edison's device came first, it doesn't explain what made the Lumière brothers' later achievement significant. If anything, emphasizing that Edison came first could suggest his work was more important, which contradicts the student's goal.
distractorRationale.D: This choice mentions elements from both innovations but fails to establish a meaningful connection between them or explain significance. It presents two disconnected facts (the Lumière brothers showed everyday scenes; Edison's device used a peephole) without clarifying why the Lumière brothers' approach represented an important advancement. The relationship between these details remains unclear.

passage:
While researching a paper on early film technology, a student has taken the following notes:

• The Lumière brothers held the first public film screening in Paris in 1895.
• Their films showed everyday scenes like workers leaving a factory.
• Thomas Edison had already developed a film viewing device called the Kinetoscope in 1891.
• Edison's device allowed only one person at a time to view films through a peephole.

The student wants to explain why the Lumière brothers' achievement was particularly significant. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

passageMetadata.source: Student research notes on film history
